reasoning: Polymarket clearly intends to go live in the US as quickly as possible, having just spent $112 million to acquire an already-licensed Designated Contract Market (QCX LLC) and an associated clearinghouse in July 2025. The regulatory runway is clear: they have resolved their past issues with the CFTC, and QCX itself received CFTC approval to operate on July 9, 2025. Since the acquisition, Polymarket has wasted no time, officially submitting updated exchange rulebooks, risk disclosures, and privacy policies to the CFTC under the name "QCX LLC, d/b/a Polymarket US" in mid-August, with effective dates listed as August 26, 2025. With a functionally licensed platform and rule changes already becoming effective in August, Polymarket has over four months remaining in 2025. Executing a single real-money trade (even in a beta environment) requires very little technical runway once regulatory approval is met. Financial regulators generally allow DCMs to "self-certify" new predictable event contracts overnight, so they won't necessarily be held up waiting for individual contract approvals. Given the rapid pace of their filings and intense financial motivation to monetize the acquisition, there is an 88% chance a single real-money trade will occur before the end of the year.
